top_categories.workout_context: 11,811ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 84, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
141ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 84 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 84 miles.
11,811ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 11,811ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: June in Vaison-la-Romaine, Vaucluse, France (Mont Ventoux summit finish) can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux’s 84 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux with 11,811ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 84 miles of mixed terrain in Vaison-la-Romaine, Vaucluse, France (Mont Ventoux summit finish).
